extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter won the hilly finish in Colmar after positioning perfectly on MATTHEWS' wheel through the final kilometer, beating TRENTIN and VAN AERT in the sprint.
tactical_patterns: Sagan deliberately sat fifth wheel through final kilometer rather than leading out, conserving energy for late acceleration.
Breakaway caught with only 22km remaining despite cooperative rotation, suggesting GC teams preferred controlled finish over gambling.
Deceuninck Quick-Step attempted dual leadout for both Alaphilippe stage hunt and time bonus protection simultaneously.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Yellow jersey attempted to contest finish sprint despite being climber, showing aggressive defense through bonus seconds
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: Failed to convert his best-ever Tour stage opportunity despite perfect positioning entering final kilometer
rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: positive: European champion nearly beat Sagan in photo finish without dedicated leadout train
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Took third place and 4-second bonus (later awarded), mixing it with pure sprinters after categorized climbs
